mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.5 percent, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning among institutional investors, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold crossings show no statistically significant difference in forward returns compared to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in consumer credit balances signals a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate revenue growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or individual series outperform the combined interaction term in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, elevated inflation readings decouple from real growth expectations, forcing the market to price in higher terminal rates and negative equity valuations over the subsequent five trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline significantly following rate hikes when inflation is above 3.0%, or the signal produces positive returns in > 50% of tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding capacity utilization and healthy aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or statistically insignificant over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns show no positive correlation with industrial output growth.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 800 thousand units, the construction sector contraction signals weakening consumer confidence and credit availability, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts breach the 800k threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises indicates a large labor force participation expansion that dilutes wage pressure, prompting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to generate a positive Rank IC above 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window, or the signal reverses direction during periods of tight labor markets.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, rapid acceleration in real GDP growth combined with stable inflation compresses risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive drift following GDP acceleration episodes, or the signal produces negative returns in > 55% of qualifying quarters.
